tax: Placed at family rank, Smouldering Pyrics is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a low-oxygen smouldering body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a smouldering body with its own detritus fuel.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a creeping glow-front in mat. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a creeping ash-band — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Smouldering Pyrics occupies fungal mats and dust-floors. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a spark smoulders in low-oxygen detritus and recur with mat regrows and re-seeds the smoulder, sustaining themselves on slow low-oxygen combustion.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: creeps unseen, flares to flame on a draught. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: smouldering body and near-invisibility. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: hidden spread and air-triggered flare. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Smouldering Pyrics matters because burns crews from below before being seen. It lets survey reports sort smouldering hazards from open-flame ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Smouldering Pyrics is the family-rank grouping of low-oxygen creeping-combustion fire elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a hidden smoulder body that creeps through detritus and flares on air, and divide below into genus groups by fuel bed and oxygen level. Patient and hidden, the opposite of the open-flame kin.
